# MSBuild Project Review тАФ ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Summary

| Project | SDK | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `ContosoApp.Api` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web` | ASP.NET Core Web API |
| `ContosoApp.Core` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` | Class library |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` | Class library |
| `ContosoApp.Api.Tests` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` | xUnit test project |
| `ContosoApp.Core.Tests` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` | xUnit test project |

No `Directory.Build.props`, `Directory.Build.targets`, `Directory.Packages.props`, `global.json`, or `nuget.config` found.

---

## ЁЯФ┤ Errors (2)

### E1 тАФ Inconsistent `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` version across projects
| Project | Version |
|---|---|
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Api` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** |

`Services` references `8.0.1` while `Core` and `Api` reference `8.0.0`. Since `Api` has a `ProjectReference` to both `Services` and `Core`, NuGet will resolve this at restore time by selecting the highest version (`8.0.1`), but the declarations are inconsistent. This is a maintenance hazard тАФ it's unclear whether `8.0.1` was intentionally chosen for `Services` or if someone forgot to update the others. If a future version introduces a behavioral change, the inconsistency makes it hard to audit.

**Fix:** Unify to a single version (addressed by CPM recommendation below).

---

### E2 тАФ Inconsistent test infrastructure package versions between test projects
| Package | `Api.Tests` | `Core.Tests` |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.9.0** | **17.10.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.7.1** | **2.8.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.5.6** | **2.8.1** |
| `NSubstitute` | 5.1.0 | 5.1.0 |

The two test projects use different major-minor versions of the test SDK and xUnit. This can cause test discovery/execution discrepancies between projects. `Api.Tests` appears to have been created earlier and never updated.

**Fix:** Unify to the latest versions (addressed by CPM recommendation below).

---

## ЁЯЯб Warnings (5)

### W1 тАФ Massive property duplication across all 5 projects (тЖТ `Directory.Build.props`)

These **7 properties** are copy-pasted identically into every single `.csproj`:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

That's **35 duplicated lines** (7 ├Ч 5 projects). When you need to change the target framework or any of these values, you must edit every file тАФ and inevitably one will be missed.

**Fix:** Create `Directory.Build.props` at the repo root:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Then remove these 7 properties from all 5 `.csproj` files.

---

### W2 тАФ `DocumentationFile` restates the SDK default path (тЖТ use `GenerateDocumentationFile`)

All 3 `src/` projects set:
```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

This is the **exact default path** the SDK uses when documentation generation is enabled. The modern, idiomatic approach is:
```xml
<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
```

This is shorter, clearer in intent, and doesn't hardcode a path that's already the default.

**Fix:** Replace `<DocumentationFile>тАж</DocumentationFile>` with `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` in all 3 src projects тАФ or better yet, add it to `Directory.Build.props` with a condition so test projects don't generate docs:
```xml
<GenerateDocumentationFile Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
```

---

### W3 тАФ `PrintBuildInfo` target duplicated across 3 src projects (тЖТ `Directory.Build.targets`)

This identical target appears in `Api.csproj`, `Core.csproj`, and `Services.csproj`:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**Fix:** Create `Directory.Build.targets` at the repo root:
```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

Then remove the target from all 3 `.csproj` files. This also gives the test projects the same diagnostic message, which is likely desirable.

---

### W4 тАФ No Central Package Management (scattered versions)

Package versions are declared inline in each `.csproj`. Across 5 projects, there are **11 distinct `PackageReference` entries with `Version` attributes**. This is the #1 cause of the version-drift bugs found in E1 and E2.

**Fix:** Introduce `Directory.Packages.props` at the repo root:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <!-- Test -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then remove the `Version="тАж"` attribute from every `<PackageReference>` in all `.csproj` files, leaving just `Include="тАж"`.

---

### W5 тАФ `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` is redundant in the Web API project

`ContosoApp.Api` uses `Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web`, which includes `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` as part of the ASP.NET Core shared framework. The explicit `<PackageReference>` is unnecessary and could cause version conflicts if the referenced version diverges from the framework's bundled version.

**Fix:** Remove `<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" тАж />` from `ContosoApp.Api.csproj`. The type is already available via the shared framework.

---

## ЁЯФ╡ Suggestions (5)

### S1 тАФ Verify whether `System.Text.Json` PackageReference is needed

`System.Text.Json` is an inbox assembly in `net8.0` (ships with the runtime at version 8.0.0). Both `Core` and `Api` reference version `8.0.5`. If this is intentional (e.g., to pick up a security patch), document that with an XML comment. If not, the references are redundant and can be removed тАФ the framework-provided version will be used automatically.

---

### S2 тАФ Add `global.json` to pin the SDK version

There is no `global.json` in the repo root. Without one, builds use whatever SDK happens to be installed on the machine, which can cause "works on my machine" issues. Recommended:
```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "8.0.400",
    "rollForward": "latestPatch"
  }
}
```

---

### S3 тАФ Consolidate split `<PropertyGroup>` blocks in src projects

`Api.csproj`, `Core.csproj`, and `Services.csproj` each have two separate `<PropertyGroup>` blocks тАФ the second one contains only `<DocumentationFile>`. There's no reason for the split (no conditions differ). Merge them into a single `<PropertyGroup>` for readability.

---

### S4 тАФ Add `IsPackable` to `Directory.Build.props` with a test-project override

Both test projects explicitly set `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>`. Once `Directory.Build.props` exists, you could add a conditional default:
```xml
<IsPackable Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">false</IsPackable>
```
This eliminates the need for test projects to declare it.

---

### S5 тАФ Consider adding `<PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>` to test runner packages

`xunit.runner.visualstudio` is a test infrastructure package that should not flow to consuming projects. While test projects are already `IsPackable=false` (so this has no practical effect today), adding `PrivateAssets="all"` is a defensive best practice:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
```

---

## ЁЯЯв Positive Findings

| Practice | Status |
|---|---|
| All projects use SDK-style format | тЬЕ |
| `Nullable` enabled everywhere | тЬЕ |
| `ImplicitUsings` enabled everywhere | тЬЕ |
| `TreatWarningsAsErrors` enabled everywhere | тЬЕ |
| `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` enabled everywhere | тЬЕ |
| Test projects marked `IsPackable=false` | тЬЕ |
| Proper use of `ProjectReference` (no assembly references) | тЬЕ |
| No hardcoded paths | тЬЕ |
| No `Exec` tasks where built-in tasks exist | тЬЕ |
| No `packages.config` (all PackageReference) | тЬЕ |
| No legacy non-SDK project files | тЬЕ |

---

## Recommended End-State File Structure

After applying all recommendations, the project files would look like this:

**New files to create:**
```
/Directory.Build.props
/Directory.Build.targets
/Directory.Packages.props
/global.json
```

**Resulting `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` (example тАФ slimmed from 27 to 8 lines):**

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />

  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**Resulting `ContosoApp.Api.Tests.csproj` (example):**
```xml

    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Api\ContosoApp.Api.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

---

## Summary

| Severity | Count | Key Theme |
|---|---|---|
| ЁЯФ┤ Error | 2 | Package version inconsistencies (drift between projects) |
| ЁЯЯб Warning | 5 | Duplication (35 duplicated property lines), no CPM, redundant package ref |
| ЁЯФ╡ Suggestion | 5 | SDK defaults restated, missing `global.json`, minor cleanups |
| ЁЯЯв Positive | 11 | Strong modern foundation тАФ all SDK-style, good quality settings |

**Highest-impact fix:** Introducing `Directory.Build.props` + `Directory.Packages.props` would eliminate all duplication and version-drift issues in one move, reducing total `.csproj` line count by roughly **60%** and making future TFM upgrades a single-line change.
